Name: Project
Description: Rollup for the entire project

Name: Project Development
Description: Top-level summary for all project development activities including feasibility studies, permitting, land acquisition, and pre-FEED scope definition prior to full project sanction.

Name: Administration & Home Office Management
Description: Top-level cost category encompassing all administration, home office management, project controls, safety, quality, procurement, and general overhead functions supporting the project.

Name: Home Office Construction Support
Description: Summary category for all home office personnel and services providing construction project support from the corporate office, including management, administration, and technical functions.

Name: Overhead
Description: Corporate overhead labor costs allocated to the project including executive management, accounting, legal, IT, and marketing functions that indirectly support project delivery.

Name: Executive
Description: Executive-level labor allocated to the project including senior leadership involvement in project oversight, strategic decisions, client relations, and corporate governance.

Name: Accounting
Description: Accounting labor allocated to the project including financial reporting, accounts payable/receivable, payroll processing, cost tracking, and audit support for project finances.

Name: Legal
Description: Legal labor allocated to the project including contract review, claims management, dispute resolution, regulatory compliance, and legal advisory services.

Name: Information & Technology
Description: Information technology labor allocated to the project including IT infrastructure support, software deployment, network administration, and technical troubleshooting for project systems.

Name: Marketing
Description: Marketing labor allocated to the project including proposal development, client presentations, project photography/documentation, and business development activities related to the project.

Name: Management
Description: Summary category for project management labor including senior leadership responsible for overall project delivery, client interface, and strategic direction of the construction effort.

Name: Contract Manager
Description: Contract manager labor responsible for managing contractual obligations, change orders, scope modifications, commercial terms, and owner/subcontractor agreement administration.

Name: Commissioning Manager
Description: Labor costs for the Commissioning Manager responsible for planning, coordinating, and overseeing all commissioning and startup activities from the home office.

Name: Supervision
Description: Summary category for field supervision labor providing on-site oversight and direction of all construction activities across disciplines.

Name: General Field
Description: General field supervision labor including the site superintendent or general superintendent responsible for day-to-day field operations, crew coordination, and schedule execution.

Name: Administration
Description: Summary category for administrative support labor performing office management, clerical, and document control functions for the project.

Name: Office Manager
Description: Office manager labor responsible for coordinating administrative operations, managing office staff, maintaining supplies, and ensuring smooth daily function of the project office.

Name: Clerical
Description: Clerical labor providing general administrative support including data entry, filing, correspondence, phone management, and administrative assistance to project staff.

Name: Document Control Manager
Description: Document control manager labor responsible for establishing and overseeing the document management system, transmittals, revision control, and records management procedures.

Name: Document Control Clerk
Description: Document control clerk labor performing day-to-day document processing, filing, distribution, tracking of submittals, RFIs, and maintaining the project document management system.

Name: Procurement/Subcontract
Description: Summary category for procurement and subcontract management labor responsible for purchasing materials, equipment, and managing subcontractor agreements for the project.

Name: Procurement Manager
Description: Procurement manager labor responsible for overall procurement strategy, vendor qualification, purchase order management, and coordination of material deliveries to the project.

Name: Procurement Administrator
Description: Procurement administrator labor supporting the purchasing function including processing requisitions, tracking orders, expediting deliveries, and maintaining procurement records.

Name: Buyer
Description: Buyer labor responsible for sourcing materials and equipment, obtaining competitive bids, negotiating pricing, and issuing purchase orders for project materials.

Name: Subcontract Manager
Description: Subcontract manager labor responsible for subcontractor pre-qualification, bid evaluation, contract negotiation, award, and overall management of subcontractor relationships.

Name: Subcontract Administrator
Description: Subcontract administrator labor supporting subcontract management including processing pay applications, tracking insurance certificates, managing change orders, and maintaining subcontract files.

Name: Project Controls
Description: Summary category for project controls labor responsible for cost management, scheduling, progress measurement, and reporting functions that monitor and control project performance.

Name: Project Controls Manager
Description: Project controls manager labor responsible for leading the project controls team, establishing control systems, and providing integrated cost and schedule analysis to project leadership.

Name: Cost Management
Description: Scheduling labor responsible for developing, maintaining, and updating the project schedule including critical path analysis, resource loading, and progress tracking.

Name: Contracting & Procurement Controls
Description: Labor for contracting and procurement controls personnel supporting contract administration, procurement tracking, and vendor management activities from the home office.

Name: Planning & Scheduling
Description: Labor for planning and scheduling personnel responsible for developing, maintaining, and reporting on the integrated project schedule from the home office.

Name: Progress & Performance
Description: Labor for progress and performance monitoring personnel responsible for tracking physical progress, earned value, and KPI reporting from the home office.

Name: Change Management
Description: Labor for change management personnel responsible for identifying, evaluating, and processing project changes including scope, schedule, and cost impacts.

Name: Reporting & Dashboards
Description: Labor for reporting and dashboard personnel responsible for compiling, analyzing, and distributing project status reports and management dashboards from the home office.

Name: Risk Management
Description: Cost engineering labor responsible for cost estimating, budgeting, forecasting, earned value analysis, change order pricing, and financial performance reporting.

Name: Safety
Description: Summary category for safety management labor responsible for developing and enforcing the project safety program, ensuring regulatory compliance, and promoting a zero-incident culture.

Name: Safety Manager
Description: Safety manager labor responsible for developing the site-specific safety plan, conducting safety orientations, leading incident investigations, and interfacing with regulatory agencies.

Name: Safety Inspector
Description: Safety inspector labor performing daily field safety inspections, hazard identification, toolbox talk facilitation, permit auditing, and enforcement of safety standards on site.

Name: Quality
Description: Summary category for quality management labor responsible for establishing and implementing the project quality program including inspections, testing, and documentation.

Name: Quality Manager
Description: Quality manager labor responsible for developing the quality management plan, overseeing QA/QC procedures, managing non-conformance reports, and coordinating third-party inspections.

Name: Quality Inspector
Description: Quality inspector labor performing field inspections, material verification, weld inspections, concrete testing oversight, and documentation of quality records and test results.

Name: Material Control
Description: Material control labor for receiving, inventorying, storing, and tracking materials and equipment at the project site, including warehouse management and material logistics.

Name: Work Gap Allowance
Description: Summary category for non-productive labor allowances covering anticipated work gaps and schedule interruptions that reduce productive field and office hours.

Name: Sickleave
Description: Sick leave allowance for project staff including anticipated paid time off for illness and medical appointments during the project duration.

Name: Haskell Orientation
Description: Labor time allocated for Haskell corporate orientation and onboarding activities including safety orientation, company policies, and project-specific induction training for new staff.

Name: Employee Expenses
Description: Summary category for employee-related expenses including travel, housing, and other reimbursable costs incurred by project staff assigned to the job site.

Name: Home Office Travel Time
Description: Labor hours for home office staff travel time to and from the project site, including mobilization and demobilization travel for corporate-based personnel.

Name: Home Office Travel
Description: Travel expenses for home office personnel visiting the project site including airfare, ground transportation, mileage reimbursement, and associated travel costs.

Name: Staffing Housing/Camp
Description: Housing and camp costs for project staff including temporary housing, furnished apartments, man-camp accommodations, and related lodging expenses for field-assigned personnel.

Name: Client Entertainment
Description: Client entertainment expenses including business meals, site visits, project milestone events, and relationship-building activities with the owner and key stakeholders.

Name: Equipment Costs
Description: Summary category for equipment costs associated with administrative and home office support functions including office equipment, vehicles, and related assets.

Name: Consumables (used one time)
Description: Summary category for consumable items used one time in administrative operations, including office supplies, testing materials, and other single-use items.

Name: Office Consumables
Description: Office consumables including paper, toner, pens, binders, printer cartridges, and other expendable office supplies consumed during daily project administration.

Name: Drinking Water
Description: Drinking water supplies for the project office and field areas including bottled water, water cooler service, and dispensing equipment.

Name: Drug Testing Kits
Description: Drug testing kits and related supplies for pre-employment, random, and post-incident substance testing of project personnel per company and regulatory requirements.

Name: Small Tools
Description: Small tools and portable equipment used in administrative and support functions including hand tools, measurement devices, and other minor portable items.

Name: Supplies (can be reused) / Services
Description: Summary category for reusable supplies and contracted services supporting administrative operations including office equipment, communications, and general project services.

Name: Freight/Shipping/Mail
Description: Summary category for freight, shipping, and mail services including all modes of transport for project documents, materials, and equipment.

Name: Freight/Shipping/Mail
Description: General freight, shipping, and mail expenses for routine project deliveries, correspondence, and package handling across all transport modes.

Name: Ground Freight
Description: Ground freight services including trucking, courier, and standard ground shipping for materials, documents, and equipment delivered to the project site.

Name: Air Freight
Description: Air freight services for expedited shipment of time-critical materials, documents, and equipment requiring rapid delivery to the project site.

Name: Ocean Freight
Description: Ocean freight services for international or long-distance shipping of heavy or bulk materials and equipment via maritime transport to the project.

Name: Signage
Description: Project signage including safety signs, directional signs, project identification boards, regulatory postings, and temporary wayfinding signage on site.

Name: Office Space Lease
Description: Office space lease costs for the project office including rent, utilities, common area maintenance, and other occupancy expenses for temporary or permanent project office facilities.

Name: Furniture
Description: Office furniture costs including desks, chairs, filing cabinets, conference tables, and other furnishings for the project office and field trailer facilities.

Name: Landline Phone
Description: Landline telephone service costs for the project office including phone lines, handsets, voicemail systems, and monthly service charges.

Name: Cell Phone
Description: Cell phone costs for project personnel including mobile devices, monthly service plans, data charges, and accessories for staff requiring mobile communication.

Name: Internet
Description: Internet service costs for the project office and field facilities including broadband connections, Wi-Fi equipment, and monthly service charges for data connectivity.

Name: Computer
Description: Computer hardware costs for the project including desktop workstations, laptops, monitors, docking stations, and peripheral devices for project staff.

Name: Software
Description: Software costs for the project including licenses for project management, scheduling, cost control, document management, CAD, and other specialized project software applications.

Name: Copier
Description: Copier and multifunction printer costs including equipment lease/purchase, toner, maintenance agreements, and per-copy charges for project document reproduction.

Name: Waste (Dumpster)
Description: Waste disposal costs including dumpster rental, trash collection service, recycling, and general waste management for the project office and administrative areas.

Name: Security
Description: Security services for the project site and office including guard services, access control systems, surveillance equipment, badge systems, and security monitoring.

Name: Safety Supplies
Description: Safety supplies for the project including personal protective equipment (PPE), first aid kits, fire extinguishers, safety cones, barricade tape, and safety signage.

Name: Project Costs
Description: Summary category for miscellaneous project-level costs not classified under labor, materials, or standard support categories, including bonds, insurance, taxes, and contingency.

Name: Bonds
Description: Summary category for surety bond costs required for the project including performance bonds, payment bonds, and other bonding requirements.

Name: Bond
Description: General bond costs for the project including surety premiums and bonding-related administrative fees.

Name: Payment & Performance
Description: Payment and performance bond costs providing surety guarantees to the owner for completion of work and payment of subcontractors and material suppliers.

Name: Insurance
Description: Summary category for project insurance costs including all risk, general liability, and professional liability coverage required for the project.

Name: Insurance
Description: General insurance costs for the project including premiums and related administrative expenses for required project insurance coverage.

Name: All Risk
Description: All risk (builder's risk) insurance covering property damage, theft, and natural disaster losses to the project during construction.

Name: General Liability
Description: General liability insurance covering bodily injury, property damage, and personal injury claims arising from project construction operations.

Name: Professional Liability
Description: Professional liability (errors and omissions) insurance covering claims arising from design errors, engineering mistakes, or professional negligence on the project.

Name: Finance Charges
Description: Finance charges and interest costs associated with project financing, letters of credit, payment terms, and other financial instruments supporting the project.

Name: Taxes
Description: Summary category for all tax obligations associated with the project including business & occupation, property, and sales and use taxes.

Name: B&O
Description: Business and occupation (B&O) tax costs applicable to the project based on gross receipts or other jurisdictional tax assessments.

Name: Property
Description: Property tax costs for project-owned or leased assets, temporary structures, and equipment subject to local property tax assessments during construction.

Name: Sales and Use
Description: Sales and use tax costs on materials, equipment, and services purchased for the project, based on applicable state and local tax rates.

Name: Permitting
Description: Permitting costs including building permits, environmental permits, specialty trade permits, and all government-required approvals and licensing fees for the project.

Name: Engineering & Design
Description: Encompasses all engineering and design disciplines required to develop, document, and deliver the full project scope from concept through detailed design.

Name: Engineering Support
Description: Supporting functions and personnel that enable the effective delivery of engineering activities across all disciplines.

Name: Overhead
Description: Indirect costs and administrative overhead allocated to support project operations, including executive, legal, IT, and back-office functions.

Name: Executive
Description: Senior executive oversight and governance functions supporting the project, including strategic decision-making and client relationship management.

Name: Accounting
Description: Financial accounting, cost tracking, invoicing, and reporting functions supporting the project.

Name: Legal
Description: Legal counsel, contract review, regulatory compliance, and dispute resolution services for the project.

Name: Information & Technology
Description: IT infrastructure, software licensing, systems administration, and digital tools supporting project delivery.

Name: Marketing
Description: Marketing, business development, and proposal support activities associated with the project.

Name: Management
Description: Overall project and engineering management functions, including leadership, planning, and coordination across all disciplines.

Name: Contract Manager
Description: Responsible for administering prime and subcontracts, tracking obligations, managing change orders, and ensuring contractual compliance.

Name: Commissioning Manager
Description: Leads the planning and execution of commissioning and startup activities, coordinating across engineering, construction, and operations teams.

Name: Administration
Description: General administrative support functions including coordination, scheduling, correspondence, and office management.

Name: Office Manager
Description: Manages day-to-day office operations, facilities, supplies, and administrative staff for the project site or home office.

Name: Clerical
Description: Provides clerical and administrative support such as filing, data entry, correspondence, and general office tasks.

Name: Document Control Manager

Name: Document Management

Name: Procurement/Subcontract
Description: Activities and personnel related to the procurement of equipment, materials, and subcontracted services required for project execution.

Name: Procurement Manager
Description: Leads the procurement team, develops procurement strategy, manages vendor relationships, and ensures timely delivery of equipment and materials.

Name: Procurement Administrator

Name: Buyer
Description: Responsible for sourcing, negotiating, and issuing purchase orders for project equipment, materials, and services.

Name: Subcontract Manager

Name: Subcontract Administrator

Name: Project Controls

Name: Dashboards <Deliv.>

Name: Project Costs
Description: Miscellaneous project-level costs associated with demolition operations that do not fall into standard material, labor, or equipment categories.

Name: Demolition Direct Labor
Description: Summary category for all direct labor costs performing hands-on demolition work across civil, concrete, steel, building, equipment, piping, electrical, and instrumentation disciplines.

Name: Civil Demolition
Description: Direct labor for civil demolition activities including earthwork removal, foundation excavation, roadway demolition, underground utility removal, and site grading.

Name: Concrete Demolition
Description: Direct labor for concrete demolition including breaking, sawing, coring, and removal of concrete foundations, slabs, walls, piers, and elevated structures.

Name: Steel Demolition
Description: Direct labor for structural and miscellaneous steel demolition including cutting, unbolting, rigging, and removal of steel frames, platforms, supports, and handrails.

Name: Buildings Demolition
Description: Direct labor for building demolition including dismantling of walls, roofing, doors, windows, interior finishes, HVAC components, and complete structure teardown.

Name: Equipment Demolition
Description: Direct labor for mechanical equipment demolition including disconnection, unbolting, rigging, and removal of vessels, tanks, heat exchangers, pumps, and rotating machinery.

Name: Piping Demolition
Description: Direct labor for piping system demolition including disconnection, cutting, capping, removal, and disposal of process piping, utility piping, and associated supports.

Name: Electrical Demolition
Description: Direct labor for electrical system demolition including de-energization, cable pulling, conduit removal, panel dismantling, and removal of lighting, switchgear, and transformers.

Name: Instrumentation Demolition
Description: Direct labor for instrumentation demolition including removal of field instruments, control valves, tubing, junction boxes, and associated mounting hardware.

Name: Coatings Demolition
Description: Direct labor for coatings and paint system demolition including removal of protective coatings, fireproofing, lead-based paint abatement, and surface preparation for new work.

Name: Demolition Subcontracts
Description: Summary category for all subcontracted demolition work, encompassing both support services and direct demolition scopes performed by third-party contractors.

Name: Support
Description: Subcontracted support services for demolition operations including site preparation, temporary facilities, safety services, and ancillary support not performed by direct labor.

Name: Site Support
Description: Subcontracted site support services specifically for demolition areas including temporary roads, fencing, security, traffic management, and utility maintenance.

Name: Dewatering
Description: Subcontracted dewatering services including pump supply, installation, operation, and monitoring of dewatering systems in demolition excavation and work areas.

Name: Direct
Description: Subcontracted direct demolition work scope, covering all disciplines of demolition performed by third-party specialty demolition contractors.

Name: Concrete Demolition
Description: Subcontracted concrete demolition services including controlled breaking, hydraulic crushing, sawcutting, removal, hauling, and disposal of concrete structures and foundations.

Name: Steel Demolition
Description: Subcontracted steel demolition services including torch cutting, mechanical shearing, rigging, removal, and disposal or salvage of structural and miscellaneous steel.

Name: Buildings Demolition
Description: Subcontracted building demolition services including complete or selective teardown of buildings, hazardous material abatement, and debris hauling and disposal.

Name: Equipment Demolition
Description: Subcontracted equipment demolition services including disconnection, removal, disposal, or relocation of mechanical equipment, vessels, and rotating machinery.

Name: Piping Demolition
Description: Subcontracted piping demolition services including disconnection, removal, and disposal of piping systems, valves, and supports across process and utility services.

Name: Electrical Demolition
Description: Subcontracted electrical demolition services including de-energization, cable and conduit removal, switchgear dismantling, and disposal of electrical system components.

Name: Instrumentation Demolition
Description: Subcontracted instrumentation demolition services including removal of field instruments, control systems, tubing, and associated infrastructure.

Description: Subcontracted insulation demolition services including removal and disposal of thermal insulation, cladding, and associated materials from piping, equipment, and structures.

Name: Structural Steel
Description: Top-level summary code encompassing all structural steel shop fabrication activities, including materials, labor, and support costs.

Name: Structural Steel Shop Materials
Description: Covers all raw and processed materials used in structural steel shop fabrication, including structural shapes, plates, and ancillary steel items.

Name: Structural Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Top-level code for all indirect and support labor costs associated with structural steel shop fabrication operations.

Name: Structural Steel Shop Support Labor
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: General support labor costs for the structural steel shop, including supervision assistance, coordination, and miscellaneous indirect labor not captured in specific subcategories.

Name: General Foreman
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor costs for General Foreman overseeing ironworker crews in the structural steel shop fabrication environment.

Name: Ironworker
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Direct labor costs for Ironworkers performing structural steel fabrication tasks including cutting, fitting, and welding structural members.

Name: Quality
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor and costs associated with Quality Control and Quality Assurance functions within the structural steel shop, including inspection and documentation.

Name: PWHT
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor and costs for Post-Weld Heat Treatment (PWHT) operations applied to fabricated structural steel components to relieve residual stresses and meet specification requirements.

Name: NDE
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor and costs for Non-Destructive Examination (NDE) activities on fabricated structural steel, including RT, UT, MT, PT, and visual inspection.

Name: Material Control
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor and overhead costs for Material Control functions including tracking, staging, kitting, and issuing materials to the shop floor.

Name: Unload & Handle
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor costs for unloading incoming structural steel materials from delivery vehicles and handling within the shop yard or storage areas.

Name: Operators
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor costs for equipment operators running cranes, forklifts, and other material handling equipment in support of shop fabrication activities.

Name: Site Support
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Indirect labor costs for site support functions such as housekeeping, safety, and general shop maintenance that support the fabrication operation.

Name: Work Gap Allowance
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Allowance applied to direct labor hours to account for planned or unplanned work gaps including weather, shift transitions, and operational delays not separately tracked.

Name: Market Recovery
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Labor cost adjustment to account for market-driven labor rate recovery or escalation above baseline wage rates for the structural steel shop workforce.

Name: Employee Expenses
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Reimbursable employee expenses such as per diem, travel, lodging, and meals for craft and staff assigned to the structural steel shop fabrication scope.

Name: Equipment Costs
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Costs associated with equipment used in the structural steel shop, including rental, mobilization, fuel, and maintenance of cranes, manipulators, and welding machines.

Name: Haskell Owned Equipment
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Costs specifically associated with Haskell-owned equipment deployed in the structural steel shop, tracked separately for internal cost recovery and asset utilization reporting.

Name: Consumables
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Costs for consumable items used during fabrication such as welding wire, electrodes, grinding discs, and cutting gases that are expended in the fabrication process.

Name: Small Tools
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Costs for small tools and hand tools used by craft workers in the shop, including purchase, rental, and maintenance of items below the capitalization threshold.

Name: Supplies (can be reused) / Services
Account Description: Structural Steel Shop Fabrication Support Labor
Description: Costs for supplies that can be reused across multiple work packages (e.g., clamps, jigs, fixtures) and external services procured to support shop fabrication.

Name: Structural Shop Fabricaiton Labor
Description: Top-level code for all direct labor costs associated with hands-on structural steel fabrication, including cutting, fitting, welding, and assembly operations.

Name: Fabricate Steel
Description: Direct labor for fabricating general structural steel members including beams, columns, braces, and connections per engineering drawings and specifications.

Name: Fabricate Module Steel
Description: Direct labor for fabricating structural steel components specifically designed for modular construction, including module frames, skid bases, and integration steel.

Name: Fabricate Pipe Racks & Sleepers
Description: Direct labor for fabricating pipe racks and pipe sleepers, including main structural members, cross-members, and connection details per piping layout drawings.

Name: Fabricate Pipe Supports
Description: Direct labor for fabricating pipe supports and hangers, including U-bolts, resting supports, guides, anchors, and spring support assemblies.

Name: Fabricate Platforms
Description: Direct labor for fabricating access platforms, including floor beams, grating frames, and structural connections for elevated work areas.

Name: Fabricate Handrail
Description: Direct labor for fabricating handrail systems including top rails, mid rails, posts, and end returns per OSHA and project specification requirements.

Name: Fabricate Toe-plate
Description: Direct labor for fabricating toe-plate (kick plate) components attached to platform edges and walkways to prevent tools or materials from falling.

Name: Fabricate Ladders & Cages
Description: Direct labor for fabricating ladders and associated safety cages used for personnel access to elevated structures and equipment.

Name: Fabricate Stair Treads
Description: Direct labor for fabricating stair treads and associated structural components for stairway assemblies providing personnel access to elevated platforms.

Name: Fabricate Safety Gates
Description: Direct labor for fabricating safety gates installed at ladder access points, platform openings, and other locations requiring fall protection closures.

Name: Fabricate Grating
Description: Direct labor for fabricating and installing grating panels into structural frames, including cutting to fit, banding edges, and securing with grating clips.

Name: Fabricate Checkered Plate
Description: Direct labor for fabricating checkered plate (floor plate) components used for heavy-duty walking surfaces, drip pans, and equipment bases.

Name: Structural Shop Coatings & Grout
Description: Top-level summary for all structural shop-applied coatings and grout work including paint, galvanizing, grouting, and fireproofing applied to fabricated structural steel prior to delivery.

Name: Coatings
Description: Direct labor for shop-applied protective coatings on structural steel including primers, intermediate coats, and finish coats applied to specified coating system requirements.

Name: Paint
Description: Direct labor for fabricating Q Deck (metal deck) components used for structural flooring, roofing, or platform infill in steel-framed structures.

Name: Galvanize
Description: Direct labor for hot-dip galvanizing of structural steel components providing long-term corrosion protection through zinc coating prior to delivery to site.

Name: Grout Steel
Description: Direct labor for shop-applied grout at structural steel base plates, column base connections, and bearing surfaces to ensure proper load transfer and alignment.

Name: Fireproof Steel
Description: Direct labor for shop-applied fireproofing on structural steel members and connections to achieve the specified fire resistance rating prior to delivery to site.

Name: Install Structural Steel
Description: Top-level cost code rollup for all Structural Steel scope, encompassing materials, support labor, erection subcontracts, and coatings/grout.

Name: Structural Steel Field Materials
Description: Summary rollup for all Structural Steel material procurement costs (cost code 331), including primary steel, miscellaneous steel, and decking materials.

Name: Mat: Structural Steel
Description: Material cost for primary structural steel members including beams, columns, bracing, and connection plates as procured from the fabricator.

Name: Miscellaneous Steel
Description: Summary rollup for miscellaneous steel material costs (cost code 331-10), covering handrails, toe-plate, ladders, cages, and safety gates.

Name: Mat: Handrail
Description: Material cost for handrail components including top rail, mid rail, posts, and fittings as furnished for fabrication or field installation.

Name: Mat: Toe-plate
Description: Material cost for toe-plate (kick plate) steel, furnished to prevent objects from being kicked off elevated platforms and walkways.

Name: Mat: Ladders & Cages
Description: Material cost for fixed steel ladders and associated safety cage components used for personnel access to elevated structures.

Name: Mat: Safety Gates
Description: Material cost for safety gate assemblies installed at ladder access points and platform openings to prevent falls.

Name: Decking
Description: Summary rollup for all decking material costs (cost code 331-20), covering grating, checkered plate, Q-Deck, and associated hardware.

Name: Mat: Grating
Description: Material cost for steel bar grating panels used for walkways, platforms, and flooring where drainage or ventilation is required.

Name: Mat: Checkered Plate
Description: Material cost for checkered (diamond) plate steel panels used for solid flooring surfaces on platforms, stairs, and walkways.

Name: Mat: Q Deck
Description: Material cost for Q-Deck (composite metal deck) panels used as a permanent form and structural element for concrete-filled floor slabs.

Name: Mat: Hardware
Description: Material cost for miscellaneous hardware associated with decking installation, including clips, fasteners, edge trim, and closure strips.

Name: Structural Steel Field Support Labor
Description: Summary rollup for all Structural Steel support labor costs (cost code 332), covering craft supervision, quality, material handling, equipment, consumables, tools, and supplies.

Name: General Foreman
Description: Labor cost for General Foreman overseeing structural steel ironworker crews, responsible for daily planning, crew coordination, and safety on the steel erection scope.

Name: Ironworker

Name: Basic Site Materials & Methods

Name: Site Preparation

Name: Tunneling, Boring & Jacking

Name: Foundation & L.B. Elements
Description: Below-grade structural support systems including spread footings, pile caps, grade beams, drilled piers, and other foundation elements transferring building loads to soil or rock.

Name: Utility Services
Description: Underground utility connections including water, sewer, gas, electrical duct bank, and telecommunications from site mains to the building service entrance.

Name: Drainage & Containment
Description: Storm drainage piping, catch basins, retention/detention systems, oil-water separators, and secondary containment structures managing site stormwater.

Name: Bases, Ballasts, Pavements & Appurtances
Description: Aggregate sub-base, crushed stone ballast, asphalt or concrete paving, curb and gutter, and appurtenant hardscape features around the building perimeter.

Name: Site Improvements & Amenities
Description: Site furnishings, pedestrian walkways, bicycle racks, benches, fencing, exterior lighting, and other amenities enhancing the project's built environment.

Name: Planting
Description: Trees, shrubs, ground cover, sod, seed, and other plant materials per the landscape design, including topsoil preparation, mulching, and initial watering.

Name: Site Restoration & Rehab
Description: Revegetation, erosion control blankets, seeding, and environmental remediation of construction-disturbed areas following project completion.

Name: Concrete
Description: All concrete work for the building structure including formwork, reinforcement, placement, finishing, and curing of cast-in-place and precast systems.

Name: Concrete Forms & Accessories
Description: Forming systems (wood, steel, aluminum, foam), shoring, reshoring, and embedded items such as anchor bolts, inserts, and blockouts used to shape concrete pours.

Name: Concrete Reinforcement
Description: Mild steel rebar (ASTM A615), epoxy-coated bars, welded wire reinforcement, and post-tensioning tendons providing tensile capacity to concrete structural members.

Name: Cast-In-Place Concrete
Description: Field-batched or ready-mix concrete placed in slabs, walls, columns, beams, and mat foundations, including pump placement, vibration, finishing, and curing.

Name: Precast Concrete
Description: Factory-manufactured concrete elements including prestressed double-tees, hollow-core planks, wall panels, and structural beams delivered and erected in the field.

Name: Cementitious Decks and Underlay
Description: Lightweight insulating concrete fills, gypsum-based self-leveling underlayments, and composite deck toppings providing structural or leveling substrate over steel or precast decks.

Name: Grouts
Description: Non-shrink, cementitious, and epoxy grout materials for base plate bearing, anchor bolt setting, equipment pad leveling, and void filling in structural applications.

Name: Concrete Restoration & Cleaning
Description: Concrete crack injection, spall repair, carbonation neutralization, surface patching, and protective overlay systems restoring structural integrity and appearance.

Name: Basic Masonry Mat. & Methods
Description: Mortar types, joint tooling, tolerances, coursing requirements, and standard construction methods governing all masonry work in the building.

Name: Stone
Description: Dimension stone cladding, natural and manufactured stone veneer, stone flooring, and monumental stone elements used for structural or architectural applications.

Name: Refractories

Name: Masonry Assemblies

Name: Masonry Restor. & Cleaning
Description: Pressure washing, chemical cleaning, tuckpointing of deteriorated joints, crack repair, and waterproofing sealers for existing and new masonry surfaces.

Name: Building Metals

Name: Expansion Control
Description: Pre-compressed foam, cover plates, and mechanical joint assemblies accommodating thermal movement, live load deflection, and seismic displacement at structural joints.

Name: Wood & Plastics

Name: Rough Carpentry

Description: Shop-fabricated cabinetry, countertops, reception desks, wall paneling, built-in shelving, and specialty millwork items meeting AWI quality standards.

Name: Structural Plastics

Name: Plastic Fabrication
Description: Custom FRP ductwork, enclosures, equipment housings, and architectural trim elements fabricated to project-specific shapes and chemical resistance requirements.

Name: Thermal & Moisture Protection
